#main v2
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# COMBINED TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOTS (Type 3) - All Groups Together
# ============================================================================
# This script performs DESeq2 analysis for timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1)
# within each variable level and group, and then creates combined barplots
# showing significant species for all groups in the same plot.
# Features:
# - Log fold change shrinkage (ashr)
# - Top 20 positive and top 20 negative logFC species (up to 40 total)
# - Ordered: most positive at top, most negative at bottom
# - Legend: "Enriched in -2" (positive LFC, regular colors) and "Decreased in -2" (negative LFC, light colors)
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(DESeq2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(speedyseq)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting combined timepoint analysis...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "combined_timepoints_plot"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

# Ensure factor levels are correct
sample_data(ps)$Batch <- as.factor(sample_data(ps)$Batch)
sample_data(ps)$Group <- as.factor(sample_data(ps)$Group)

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

# Clean taxonomy: remove prefixes and pipe suffixes
clean_tax_table <- function(ps_obj) {
  tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(tax_table(ps_obj)))
  for(col in colnames(tax_table_df)) {
    tax_table_df[[col]] <- gsub("\\|.*$", "", tax_table_df[[col]])
    tax_table_df[[col]] <- gsub("^[a-z]__", "", tax_table_df[[col]])
  }
  tax_table(ps_obj) <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax_table_df))
  return(ps_obj)
}
ps <- clean_tax_table(ps)

# Aggregate to Species level
cat("Aggregating taxa at Species level...\n")
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Species", NArm = TRUE)
cat("After tax_glom: ", ntaxa(ps), " unique species\n")

# Filter core taxa (detection 1%, prevalence 10%)
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
core_taxa <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_filtered <- prune_taxa(core_taxa, ps)
cat("Filtered taxa count:", ntaxa(ps_filtered), "\n")

# Define colors for groups
group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00")
lighten_color <- function(color, factor = 0.6) {
  col <- col2rgb(color)
  light_col <- col + (255 - col) * factor
  rgb(light_col[1], light_col[2], light_col[3], maxColorValue = 255)
}
group_colors_light <- sapply(group_colors, lighten_color)
names(group_colors_light) <- names(group_colors)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: HELPER FUNCTION TO PREPARE DESeqDataSet (no batch correction)
# ============================================================================
prepare_dds <- function(ps_obj, design_formula) {
  counts <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  coldata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  cat("        Count matrix shape:", nrow(counts), "taxa,", ncol(counts), "samples\n")
  cat("        Any NA in counts:", any(is.na(counts)), "\n")
  cat("        Any infinite in counts:", any(is.infinite(counts)), "\n")
  
  # Replace NA and infinite with 0
  if (any(is.na(counts))) {
    cat("        Found", sum(is.na(counts)), "NA values. Replacing with 0.\n")
    counts[is.na(counts)] <- 0
  }
  if (any(is.infinite(counts))) {
    cat("        Found", sum(is.infinite(counts)), "infinite values. Replacing with 0.\n")
    counts[is.infinite(counts)] <- 0
  }
  
  # Ensure non-negative
  if (any(counts < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    cat("        Found negative counts. Setting to 0.\n")
    counts[counts < 0] <- 0
  }
  
  # Round to integers if not already
  if (!all(counts == floor(counts))) {
    cat("        Non-integer counts found, rounding...\n")
    counts <- round(counts)
  }
  
  # Ensure taxa are rows
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) {
    cat("        Transposing count matrix to have taxa as rows.\n")
    counts <- t(counts)
  }
  
  # Build DESeqDataSet
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                                colData = coldata,
                                design = design_formula)
  cat("        DESeqDataSet created successfully\n")
  return(dds)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: ANALYSIS FUNCTION – Timepoints within Level & Group (with lfcShrink)
# ============================================================================
analyze_timepoints_within_level_group <- function(ps_obj, variable, var_levels, min_samples = 5) {
  # Returns a list: for each group (A, B, C), a data frame of significant species
  # with columns: Species, Mean_LogFC (shrunken), signif, Group, Direction
  results_list <- list()
  sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # For each variable level
  for (var_level in var_levels) {
    cat("    Level:", var_level, "\n")
    level_samples <- sample_df[[variable]] == var_level & !is.na(sample_df[[variable]])
    if (sum(level_samples) < min_samples * 2) {
      cat("        Insufficient samples\n")
      next
    }
    ps_level <- prune_samples(level_samples, ps_obj)
    
    # For each group
    for (group in c("A", "B", "C")) {
      cat("      Group:", group, "\n")
      group_samples <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_level))
      group_samples <- group_samples$Group == group
      if (sum(group_samples) < min_samples * 2) {
        cat("        Insufficient samples\n")
        next
      }
      ps_group <- prune_samples(group_samples, ps_level)
      group_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_group))
      
      # Ensure Timepoint is factor with correct levels
      group_df$Timepoint_Factor <- factor(group_df$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))
      sample_data(ps_group) <- sample_data(group_df)
      
      # Check timepoint distribution
      timepoint_counts <- table(group_df$Timepoint_Factor)
      if (all(timepoint_counts >= min_samples) && length(timepoint_counts) == 2) {
        cat("        Timepoints: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
        
        tryCatch({
          dds <- prepare_dds(ps_group, ~ Timepoint_Factor)
          dds <- DESeq(dds, test = "Wald", fitType = "local", sfType = "poscounts", quiet = TRUE)
          
          # Use lfcShrink for stable log fold changes
          res_shrink <- lfcShrink(dds, contrast = c("Timepoint_Factor", "-2", "-1"),
                                  type = "ashr", quiet = TRUE)
          
          # Extract results
          sigtab <- as.data.frame(res_shrink)
          sigtab <- sigtab[which(sigtab$padj <= 0.05 & !is.na(sigtab$padj)), ]
          sigtab <- sigtab[which(abs(sigtab$log2FoldChange) > 1), ]
          
          if (nrow(sigtab) > 0) {
            # Add taxonomy info
            tax_info <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_group)[rownames(sigtab), ])
            sigtab <- cbind(sigtab, tax_info)
            sigtab$Species <- gsub("\\|.*$", "", sigtab$Species)
            sigtab <- sigtab[!is.na(sigtab$Species) & sigtab$Species != "", ]
            
            # Prepare plot data (using shrunken LFC)
            plot_data <- data.frame(
              Species = sigtab$Species,
              Mean_LogFC = sigtab$log2FoldChange,
              signif = case_when(
                sigtab$padj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
                sigtab$padj <= 0.01 ~ "**",
                sigtab$padj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
                TRUE ~ ""
              ),
              Group = group,
              Direction = ifelse(sigtab$log2FoldChange > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
            )
            
            # Save individual group results to CSV
            clean_name <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", paste(variable, var_level, "Group", group, sep = "_"))
            write.csv(sigtab, file.path(output_dir, paste0("type3_", clean_name, ".csv")), row.names = TRUE)
            
            # Store for combined plot
            key <- paste(variable, var_level, group, sep = "_")
            results_list[[key]] <- plot_data
            cat("        Found", nrow(plot_data), "significant species (shrunken LFC)\n")
          } else {
            cat("        No significant species found\n")
          }
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat("        Error:", e$message, "\n")
        })
      } else {
        cat("        Insufficient samples in both timepoints\n")
      }
    }
  }
  return(results_list)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: PLOTTING FUNCTION – Combined Timepoint Comparison (with top 20/20 and correct ordering)
# ============================================================================
create_combined_timepoint_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                          group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined Type 3 plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative logFC
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC < 0)
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending logFC
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_logFC = max(Mean_LogFC), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_logFC)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending logFC (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_logFC = min(Mean_LogFC), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_logFC) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # --- Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending ---
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_LogFC)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_LogFC)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness: positive species first, then negative species not already in positive
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  
  # REVERSE the order so that most positive appears at the top of the plot
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply the factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add a fill group factor combining Group and Direction
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(
      FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_")
    )
  
  # Define color palette for each group and direction
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Set factor levels for FillGroup
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(
      Group = factor(Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C")),
      FillGroup = factor(FillGroup, levels = names(color_palette))
    )
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_LogFC, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_LogFC >= 0,
                             Mean_LogFC + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_LogFC)),
                             Mean_LogFC - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_LogFC)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1):", title),
      x = "Species",
      y = "Log2 Fold Change (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 16
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 6: MAIN ANALYSIS LOOP
# ============================================================================

cat("\nStarting combined timepoint analysis...\n")

# Define the variable to analyze (you can change this list)
categorical_vars <- c("BMI_group","Antibiotics_Last_Year", "Proton_Pump_Inhibitors","Hypoglycemic_Drugs","BMI_group")
# For each variable
for (variable in categorical_vars) {
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("Variable:", variable, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  
  # Get unique levels from metadata
  sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_filtered))
  if (!(variable %in% colnames(sample_df))) {
    cat("Variable not found in sample data. Skipping.\n")
    next
  }
  var_levels <- unique(sample_df[[variable]])
  var_levels <- var_levels[!is.na(var_levels)]
  cat("Levels:", paste(var_levels, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Run analysis for this variable
  results <- analyze_timepoints_within_level_group(ps_filtered, variable, var_levels, min_samples = 5)
  
  # Now create combined plots for each level
  cat("\nCreating combined plots...\n")
  for (var_level in var_levels) {
    # Collect data from all groups for this level
    combined_data <- data.frame()
    for (group in c("A", "B", "C")) {
      key <- paste(variable, var_level, group, sep = "_")
      if (!is.null(results[[key]])) {
        combined_data <- rbind(combined_data, results[[key]])
      }
    }
    if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
      clean_name <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", paste(variable, var_level, sep = "_"))
      title <- paste(variable, "-", var_level)
      create_combined_timepoint_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
                                     group_colors, group_colors_light)
    } else {
      cat("  No data for level:", var_level, "\n")
    }
  }
}

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```


#main v3(no grouping by categorical_vars)
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# COMBINED TIMEPOINT COMPARISON PLOTS (Type 3) - All Groups Together
# ============================================================================
# This script performs DESeq2 analysis for timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1)
# within each variable level and group, and then creates combined barplots
# showing significant species for all groups in the same plot.
# Features:
# - Log fold change shrinkage (ashr)
# - Top 20 positive and top 20 negative logFC species (up to 40 total)
# - Ordered: most positive at top, most negative at bottom
# - Legend: "Enriched in -2" (positive LFC, regular colors) and "Decreased in -2" (negative LFC, light colors)
# 
# NEW: Global analysis (no categorical variable) – compare timepoints per group
#      and produce combined + individual plots.
# ============================================================================

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(DESeq2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(speedyseq)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 1: SETUP
# ============================================================================

cat("Starting combined timepoint analysis...\n")

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "combined_timepoints_plot"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# Set up logging
log_file <- file.path(output_dir, "analysis_log.txt")
sink(log_file, split = TRUE)
cat("Analysis started at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 2: DATA PREPARATION
# ============================================================================

# Read the phyloseq object (adjust path if needed)
ps <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

# Ensure factor levels are correct
sample_data(ps)$Batch <- as.factor(sample_data(ps)$Batch)
sample_data(ps)$Group <- as.factor(sample_data(ps)$Group)

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

# Clean taxonomy: remove prefixes and pipe suffixes
clean_tax_table <- function(ps_obj) {
  tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(tax_table(ps_obj)))
  for(col in colnames(tax_table_df)) {
    tax_table_df[[col]] <- gsub("\\|.*$", "", tax_table_df[[col]])
    tax_table_df[[col]] <- gsub("^[a-z]__", "", tax_table_df[[col]])
  }
  tax_table(ps_obj) <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax_table_df))
  return(ps_obj)
}
ps <- clean_tax_table(ps)

# Aggregate to Species level
cat("Aggregating taxa at Species level...\n")
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Species", NArm = TRUE)
cat("After tax_glom: ", ntaxa(ps), " unique species\n")

# Filter core taxa (detection 1%, prevalence 10%)
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
core_taxa <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = FALSE)
ps_filtered <- prune_taxa(core_taxa, ps)
cat("Filtered taxa count:", ntaxa(ps_filtered), "\n")

# Define colors for groups
group_colors <- c("A" = "#FF0000", "B" = "#0000FF", "C" = "#00FF00")
lighten_color <- function(color, factor = 0.6) {
  col <- col2rgb(color)
  light_col <- col + (255 - col) * factor
  rgb(light_col[1], light_col[2], light_col[3], maxColorValue = 255)
}
group_colors_light <- sapply(group_colors, lighten_color)
names(group_colors_light) <- names(group_colors)

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 3: HELPER FUNCTION TO PREPARE DESeqDataSet (no batch correction)
# ============================================================================
prepare_dds <- function(ps_obj, design_formula) {
  counts <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  coldata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  cat("        Count matrix shape:", nrow(counts), "taxa,", ncol(counts), "samples\n")
  cat("        Any NA in counts:", any(is.na(counts)), "\n")
  cat("        Any infinite in counts:", any(is.infinite(counts)), "\n")
  
  # Replace NA and infinite with 0
  if (any(is.na(counts))) {
    cat("        Found", sum(is.na(counts)), "NA values. Replacing with 0.\n")
    counts[is.na(counts)] <- 0
  }
  if (any(is.infinite(counts))) {
    cat("        Found", sum(is.infinite(counts)), "infinite values. Replacing with 0.\n")
    counts[is.infinite(counts)] <- 0
  }
  
  # Ensure non-negative
  if (any(counts < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    cat("        Found negative counts. Setting to 0.\n")
    counts[counts < 0] <- 0
  }
  
  # Round to integers if not already
  if (!all(counts == floor(counts))) {
    cat("        Non-integer counts found, rounding...\n")
    counts <- round(counts)
  }
  
  # Ensure taxa are rows
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) {
    cat("        Transposing count matrix to have taxa as rows.\n")
    counts <- t(counts)
  }
  
  # Build DESeqDataSet
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                                colData = coldata,
                                design = design_formula)
  cat("        DESeqDataSet created successfully\n")
  return(dds)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 4: GLOBAL TIMEPOINT ANALYSIS (no categorical variable)
# ============================================================================
analyze_global_timepoints <- function(ps_obj, min_samples = 5) {
  # Returns a list: for each group (A, B, C), a data frame of significant species
  # with columns: Species, Mean_LogFC (shrunken), signif, Group, Direction
  results_list <- list()
  sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj))
  
  # For each group, run analysis on all samples of that group (no additional stratification)
  for (group in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    cat("\n  Global analysis - Group:", group, "\n")
    group_samples <- sample_df$Group == group
    if (sum(group_samples) < min_samples * 2) {
      cat("        Insufficient samples\n")
      next
    }
    ps_group <- prune_samples(group_samples, ps_obj)
    group_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_group))
    
    # Ensure Timepoint is factor with correct levels
    group_df$Timepoint_Factor <- factor(group_df$Timepoint, levels = c("-1", "-2"))
    sample_data(ps_group) <- sample_data(group_df)
    
    # Check timepoint distribution
    timepoint_counts <- table(group_df$Timepoint_Factor)
    if (all(timepoint_counts >= min_samples) && length(timepoint_counts) == 2) {
      cat("        Timepoints: -1 =", timepoint_counts["-1"], ", -2 =", timepoint_counts["-2"], "\n")
      
      tryCatch({
        dds <- prepare_dds(ps_group, ~ Timepoint_Factor)
        dds <- DESeq(dds, test = "Wald", fitType = "local", sfType = "poscounts", quiet = TRUE)
        
        # Use lfcShrink for stable log fold changes
        res_shrink <- lfcShrink(dds, contrast = c("Timepoint_Factor", "-2", "-1"),
                                type = "ashr", quiet = TRUE)
        
        # Extract results
        sigtab <- as.data.frame(res_shrink)
        sigtab <- sigtab[which(sigtab$padj <= 0.05 & !is.na(sigtab$padj)), ]
        sigtab <- sigtab[which(abs(sigtab$log2FoldChange) > 1), ]
        
        if (nrow(sigtab) > 0) {
          # Add taxonomy info
          tax_info <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_group)[rownames(sigtab), ])
          sigtab <- cbind(sigtab, tax_info)
          sigtab$Species <- gsub("\\|.*$", "", sigtab$Species)
          sigtab <- sigtab[!is.na(sigtab$Species) & sigtab$Species != "", ]
          
          # Prepare plot data
          plot_data <- data.frame(
            Species = sigtab$Species,
            Mean_LogFC = sigtab$log2FoldChange,
            signif = case_when(
              sigtab$padj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
              sigtab$padj <= 0.01 ~ "**",
              sigtab$padj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
              TRUE ~ ""
            ),
            Group = group,
            Direction = ifelse(sigtab$log2FoldChange > 0, "Enriched_in_-2", "Decreased_in_-2")
          )
          
          # Save individual group results to CSV
          clean_name <- paste0("global_Group_", group)
          write.csv(sigtab, file.path(output_dir, paste0("global_", clean_name, ".csv")), row.names = TRUE)
          
          results_list[[group]] <- plot_data
          cat("        Found", nrow(plot_data), "significant species (shrunken LFC)\n")
        } else {
          cat("        No significant species found\n")
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat("        Error:", e$message, "\n")
      })
    } else {
      cat("        Insufficient samples in both timepoints\n")
    }
  }
  return(results_list)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 5: PLOTTING FUNCTIONS (combined and individual)
# ============================================================================

# Function to create a plot for a single group (no grouping by group in the plot itself)
create_single_group_plot <- function(plot_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                     group_color, group_color_light) {
  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to plot\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating single group plot for", unique(plot_data$Group), "\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(plot_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(plot_data$Species)), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative
  pos_data <- plot_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC > 0)
  neg_data <- plot_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC < 0)
  
  # Top 20 positive and negative
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 20) {
    pos_data <- pos_data %>% arrange(desc(Mean_LogFC)) %>% slice_head(n = 20)
  }
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 20) {
    neg_data <- neg_data %>% arrange(Mean_LogFC) %>% slice_head(n = 20)
  }
  
  plot_data <- bind_rows(pos_data, neg_data)
  
  # Order: positive descending, then negative ascending, then reverse
  pos_ordered <- plot_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC > 0) %>% arrange(desc(Mean_LogFC)) %>% pull(Species)
  neg_ordered <- plot_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC < 0) %>% arrange(Mean_LogFC) %>% pull(Species)
  species_order <- rev(c(pos_ordered, neg_ordered))
  
  plot_data <- plot_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Color based on direction (regular for positive, light for negative)
  plot_data <- plot_data %>%
    mutate(FillColor = ifelse(Mean_LogFC > 0, group_color, group_color_light))
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_LogFC, fill = FillColor)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_LogFC >= 0,
                             Mean_LogFC + 0.1 * max(abs(plot_data$Mean_LogFC)),
                             Mean_LogFC - 0.1 * max(abs(plot_data$Mean_LogFC)))),
              size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_identity(guide = "legend", 
                        labels = c(group_color = "Enriched in -2", 
                                   group_color_light = "Decreased in -2"),
                        name = "Direction") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Species",
      y = "Log2 Fold Change (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  n_species <- length(unique(plot_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 14
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, ".pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, ".png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Single group plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(plot_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# Combined plot function (as before, but using the same logic)
create_combined_timepoint_plot <- function(combined_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                          group_colors, group_colors_light) {
  if (nrow(combined_data) == 0) {
    cat("        No data to combine\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("        DEBUG - Creating combined Type 3 plot\n")
  cat("        - Input rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  cat("        - Unique species:", length(unique(combined_data$Species)), "\n")
  cat("        - Groups present:", paste(unique(combined_data$Group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Separate positive and negative logFC
  pos_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC > 0)
  neg_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Mean_LogFC < 0)
  
  # Top 20 positive by descending logFC
  if (nrow(pos_data) > 0) {
    pos_ranking <- pos_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_logFC = max(Mean_LogFC), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(max_logFC)) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    pos_species <- pos_ranking$Species
  } else {
    pos_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Top 20 negative by ascending logFC (most negative first)
  if (nrow(neg_data) > 0) {
    neg_ranking <- neg_data %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_logFC = min(Mean_LogFC), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(min_logFC) %>%
      dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)
    neg_species <- neg_ranking$Species
  } else {
    neg_species <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Combine selected species (up to 40)
  selected_species <- unique(c(pos_species, neg_species))
  combined_data <- combined_data %>% filter(Species %in% selected_species)
  
  # --- Ordering: positive descending, then negative ascending ---
  if (length(pos_species) > 0) {
    pos_ordered <- pos_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% pos_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(max_fc = max(Mean_LogFC)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_fc)) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    pos_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  if (length(neg_species) > 0) {
    neg_ordered <- neg_data %>%
      filter(Species %in% neg_species) %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(min_fc = min(Mean_LogFC)) %>%
      arrange(min_fc) %>%
      pull(Species)
  } else {
    neg_ordered <- character(0)
  }
  
  # Ensure uniqueness: positive species first, then negative species not already in positive
  pos_unique <- unique(pos_ordered)
  neg_unique <- setdiff(unique(neg_ordered), pos_unique)
  species_order <- c(pos_unique, neg_unique)
  
  # REVERSE the order so that most positive appears at the top of the plot
  species_order <- rev(species_order)
  
  # Apply the factor levels
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order))
  
  # Add a fill group factor combining Group and Direction
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(
      FillGroup = paste(Group, Direction, sep = "_")
    )
  
  # Define color palette for each group and direction
  color_palette <- c()
  for (g in c("A", "B", "C")) {
    reg <- if(g == "A") "#FF0000" else if(g == "B") "#0000FF" else "#00FF00"
    light <- if(g == "A") "#FF9999" else if(g == "B") "#9999FF" else "#99FF99"
    color_palette[paste(g, "Enriched_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- reg
    color_palette[paste(g, "Decreased_in_-2", sep = "_")] <- light
  }
  
  # Set factor levels for FillGroup
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    mutate(
      Group = factor(Group, levels = c("A", "B", "C")),
      FillGroup = factor(FillGroup, levels = names(color_palette))
    )
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Species, y = Mean_LogFC, fill = FillGroup)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif,
                  y = ifelse(Mean_LogFC >= 0,
                             Mean_LogFC + 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_LogFC)),
                             Mean_LogFC - 0.1 * max(abs(combined_data$Mean_LogFC)))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 3, vjust = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name = "Group & Direction",
      values = color_palette,
      labels = c("A_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group A (enriched in -2)",
                 "A_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group A (decreased in -2)",
                 "B_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group B (enriched in -2)",
                 "B_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group B (decreased in -2)",
                 "C_Enriched_in_-2" = "Group C (enriched in -2)",
                 "C_Decreased_in_-2" = "Group C (decreased in -2)")
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10, colour = 'black'),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, colour = 'black'),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Timepoint comparisons (-2 vs -1):", title),
      x = "Species",
      y = "Log2 Fold Change (Timepoint -2 vs -1)\n(positive = enriched in -2, negative = decreased in -2)"
    )
  
  # Save plot
  n_species <- length(unique(combined_data$Species))
  plot_height <- max(8, n_species * 0.4)
  plot_width <- 16
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.pdf")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_", filename, "_combined.png")),
         p, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = 300, limitsize = FALSE)
  
  cat("        ✓ Combined plot saved with", n_species, "species (", nrow(combined_data), "bars)\n")
  return(p)
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 6: GLOBAL ANALYSIS EXECUTION (no categorical variables)
# ============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("GLOBAL TIMEPOINT ANALYSIS (no categorical variable)\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# Run global analysis
global_results <- analyze_global_timepoints(ps_filtered, min_samples = 5)

# Create individual plots for each group
for (group in c("A", "B", "C")) {
  if (!is.null(global_results[[group]])) {
    group_data <- global_results[[group]]
    # Determine colors for this group
    reg_color <- group_colors[group]
    light_color <- group_colors_light[group]
    title <- paste("Timepoint -2 vs -1 - Group", group)
    filename <- paste0("global_Group_", group)
    create_single_group_plot(group_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                             reg_color, light_color)
  } else {
    cat("No significant results for Group", group, "\n")
  }
}

# Create combined plot from all groups' results
combined_global_data <- bind_rows(global_results)
if (nrow(combined_global_data) > 0) {
  title <- "Global - All Groups"
  filename <- "global_all_groups"
  create_combined_timepoint_plot(combined_global_data, title, output_dir, filename,
                                 group_colors, group_colors_light)
} else {
  cat("No data for combined global plot.\n")
}

# ============================================================================
# SECTION 7: OPTIONAL – CATEGORICAL VARIABLES ANALYSIS (commented out)
# ============================================================================
# If you want to run the original categorical analysis, uncomment the block below.
# Otherwise, skip.

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
cat("Categorical analysis SKIPPED. To run, uncomment the code in Section 7.\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

# The original categorical loop is commented out to avoid confusion.
# To enable it, remove the comment markers.

# categorical_vars <- c("BMI_group","Antibiotics_Last_Year", "Proton_Pump_Inhibitors","Hypoglycemic_Drugs")
# for (variable in categorical_vars) {
#   cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
#   cat("Variable:", variable, "\n")
#   cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")
#   sample_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_filtered))
#   if (!(variable %in% colnames(sample_df))) {
#     cat("Variable not found in sample data. Skipping.\n")
#     next
#   }
#   var_levels <- unique(sample_df[[variable]])
#   var_levels <- var_levels[!is.na(var_levels)]
#   cat("Levels:", paste(var_levels, collapse = ", "), "\n")
#   results <- analyze_timepoints_within_level_group(ps_filtered, variable, var_levels, min_samples = 5)
#   cat("\nCreating combined plots...\n")
#   for (var_level in var_levels) {
#     combined_data <- data.frame()
#     for (group in c("A", "B", "C")) {
#       key <- paste(variable, var_level, group, sep = "_")
#       if (!is.null(results[[key]])) {
#         combined_data <- rbind(combined_data, results[[key]])
#       }
#     }
#     if (nrow(combined_data) > 0) {
#       clean_name <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", paste(variable, var_level, sep = "_"))
#       title <- paste(variable, "-", var_level)
#       create_combined_timepoint_plot(combined_data, title, output_dir, clean_name,
#                                      group_colors, group_colors_light)
#     } else {
#       cat("  No data for level:", var_level, "\n")
#     }
#   }
# }

cat("\nAnalysis completed at:", as.character(Sys.time()), "\n")
sink()
```
